hero.title: The new way to build products
hero.subtitle1: Product development used to mean manually writing code, running analysis, diagnosing bugs, and rolling out changes using dozens of tools.
hero.subtitle2: Klynt is the only platform that acts like a co-pilot for you (and your AI agents) to do it all —
hero.installHint: Install with AI in a single prompt. Paste into your terminal or code editor and make AI do the work.

tabUnderstand.title: Understand what users are doing
tabUnderstand.body: Measure engagement, track conversion, and understand usage patterns – whether it's by person, company, or AI feature.
tabUnderstand.link: How we analyze data and build products has changed a lot. Work smarter with Klynt AI to get direct answers to questions about your data.

tabData.title: Build better products with better data
tabData.body: Not your mama's data integrations. Third party data is imported into Klynt's CDP and warehouse as a first-class citizen. This means you can query third party data and product usage data, leading to more informed decisions.

tabDebug.title: Debug & fix issues faster
tabDebug.body: See exactly what went wrong with session replay, error tracking, and logs. Understand the full context of user issues without guessing.

tabShip.title: Ship features safely & get feedback
tabShip.body1: Now that you know what's happening, you can make informed decisions about what to build. Klynt helps you roll out features to specific users or groups, then test and validate ideas before you ship them to everyone.
tabShip.body2: And you can build it all with Klynt Code, our new AI code editor.

customers.title: Who's using Klynt?
customers.subtitle: Here are some of our paying customers. (Yes they actually use us, no it's not just some random engineer who tried us out 2+ years ago.)

dataStack.title: Klynt data stack, built for data teams and loved by product teams
dataStack.body1: When you're analyzing how customers use your product, you should be operating from the full set of data.
dataStack.body2: This includes customer information that happens outside your product: payments from Stripe, exceptions in an error tracking tool, tickets in your support platform.
dataStack.body3: Having all the data in one place means you can make more informed decisions about what to build next. Klynt's Product OS isn't just analytics – it's the entire suite of tools built to give you a single source of truth about your customers.

pricing.title: Usage-based pricing
pricing.philosophy: Our whole philosophy is that you shouldn't have to worry about pricing.
pricing.freeTierText: All our paid products are pay-per-use with generous monthly free tiers. In fact, 98% of our customers use Klynt for free.
pricing.salesText: We aim to match the cheapest option at scale – Klynt should be a no-brainer. You never have to "jump on a quick call" with sales.

autocapture.body: Just install Klynt and we automatically capture:

items.title: EU hosting
items.desc: Choose where your data lives. EU or US.

items.title: No third-party cookies
items.desc: We don't use third-party cookies. Ever.

items.title: Open source
items.desc: Our code is open source. Audit it yourself.

notes.title: Welcome to Product Analytics. This is our core product — the thing that started it all. Let's walk through what makes Klynt's analytics different.
notes.track: Klynt autocaptures pageviews and clicks automatically. Add custom events for deeper insights. Track the full user journey from first visit to power user.
notes.features: Six core analysis tools. Each one is designed to answer a specific type of product question. Use them individually or combine for deeper insights.
notes.autocapture: The magic of autocapture — install Klynt and data starts flowing immediately. No need to define every event upfront. Retroactively define events using our 'Actions' feature.
notes.privacy: Privacy isn't an afterthought. Choose your data region, no third-party cookies, and our code is open source. The cookie banner isn't just a joke — it's a statement.
notes.pricing: Generous free tier — 1 million events per month. Most startups never pay a cent. When you do grow, our pricing is transparent and usage-based.
notes.integration: SDKs for every major framework. React, Vue, Angular, mobile, backend — we've got you covered. Install takes less than 5 minutes.
notes.cta: That's Product Analytics. The easiest way to start is to install Klynt and let autocapture do the work. Questions? Talk to a human — we actually respond.

hero.title: Klynt Cloud
hero.body1: Klynt is designed to grow with you. Our {{productCount}} products (and counting) will take you from idea to product-market fit to IPO and beyond.
hero.body2: Our generous free tier means more than 90% of companies use Klynt for free. Only add a card if you need more than the free tier limits, advanced features, or want more projects. You still keep the same monthly free volume, even after upgrading.
hero.caption: The latest version of Klynt comes with 10+ products, generous free tiers, and usage-based pricing.

freeTier.title: Free tier on all plans
freeTier.subtitle: Our generous free tier resets monthly

scalePlan.title: Scale plan
scalePlan.subtitle: For growing teams that need more

usagePricing.title: Usage-based pricing
usagePricing.subtitle: Once you exceed your free tier, you only pay for what you use. No hidden fees, no surprises.

calculator.title: Pricing calculator
calculator.subtitle: Estimate your monthly cost based on usage
calculator.estimateNote: This is an estimate. You pay nothing until you exceed your free tier.

items.question: What happens when I exceed my free tier?
items.answer: You only pay for usage beyond the free tier. We'll never charge you without warning. You can set up billing alerts to get notified when you're approaching your limits.

items.question: Can I use Klynt for free forever?
items.answer: Yes! More than 90% of companies use Klynt for free. The free tier resets every month, so you get the same generous allowance continuously.

items.question: Do I need a credit card to start?
items.answer: No. You can use Klynt's full free tier without any payment information. We only ask for billing details when you choose to upgrade for higher limits.

items.question: What is the difference between Free and Scale plans?
items.answer: Scale adds higher usage limits, SSO/SAML authentication, advanced permissions, custom data retention, and dedicated support. The base price is $199/month plus usage.

items.question: Can I self-host Klynt?
items.answer: Yes, we offer a self-hosted option for enterprise customers who need to keep data on their own infrastructure. Contact our sales team for details.

items.question: How does usage-based pricing work?
items.answer: You pay per unit (event, session, API call) beyond your free tier. There are no flat fees or hidden charges. Pricing tiers get cheaper per unit as your volume increases.

items.question: Is there an annual discount?
items.answer: Yes, annual plans include a discount. Contact us for details on enterprise pricing and custom annual agreements.

items.question: What support do I get?
items.answer: Free plans include community support via our forums and Slack. Scale plans get priority email support. Enterprise plans include a dedicated support engineer.

hero.title: Devtools and product data infrastructure for building successful products
hero.body: Humans and AI agents build with Klynt because everything you need to collect and analyze product usage data – and build and ship new features – lives in one place.

dataPlatform.title: Data platform
dataPlatform.body: Having all your product data in one place means you can make more informed decisions. Push all your data to Klynt, then send it anywhere else you need, too.

automaticTooling.title: Automatic tooling
automaticTooling.bodyBefore: In a previous era of building products, you'd need to configure event tracking and feature flags manually. Now, your AI coding agent can use the
automaticTooling.bodyAfter: to configure Klynt without leaving your ADE.

spotlight.name: Dana
spotlight.bio: In a past life, Dana studied medicine. Now she helps teams get started with Klynt. In her downtime she's a founding member of Klynt's D&D club.

items.title: Everything (and everyone) is build mode now
items.excerpt: There's a future version of you who isn't boxed into one job function. Someone who has an idea in the morning and the means to ship it the same afternoon. Farfetched? Hardly. That version of you isn't waiting for 2030 – they exist today. They're in…

items.title: All social media metrics are bad
items.excerpt: Picture this: your company's social media team excitedly informs you that stonks are up this month. You can see the screenshots. They're green. Holy smokes. We are killing it. Social media has been solved...

items.question: How fast do you actually respond?
items.answer: We're humans, not bots — so it depends on time zones and sleep schedules. But most questions in our Discord get answered within a few hours. Email responses are typically within 24 hours.

items.question: Do you offer paid support plans?
items.answer: We don't have formal support tiers. Every user gets the same human-level support. For large enterprise deployments, we offer a dedicated success engineer as part of our Enterprise plan.

items.question: Can I schedule a demo or call?
items.answer: We don't do traditional sales calls. If you need help, just ask in Discord or send an email. We're happy to jump on a call if it's the best way to solve your problem.

items.question: I found a bug — what should I do?
items.answer: The fastest way is to open an issue on GitHub. Include steps to reproduce, expected vs actual behavior, and screenshots if relevant. We triage bugs daily.

items.question: How do I request a new feature?
items.answer: Post a feature request on GitHub Discussions or mention it in Discord. We actively read and tag every request. Many of our best features came directly from community suggestions.

header.description: Things we have deleted from the site but could not bear to destroy

detail.restoreTooltip: You can't restore from trash. That's not how trash works.

items.description: Our first logo attempt. It was... a choice.
content: [SVG IMAGE: A hedgehog drawn in MS Paint with approximately 47 poorly-aligned circles for "spines". The color palette appears to be "all of them". When shown to focus groups, 100% of participants asked "is that a potato?"]
We keep this as a reminder that design is hard, and also that we should never let James near vector graphics software.
[SVG IMAGE: An abstract "minimalist" hedgehog that looks suspiciously like a hairbrush. One intern said it looked "corporate." James took that as a compliment. It was not meant as one.]
The lesson: when someone says your logo looks "corporate," they mean it looks like a mid-2000s enterprise software company that sells printer toner.

items.description: What our job descriptions would say if we were honest
content: You'll fix CSS bugs introduced by people who don't test their changes. You'll attend meetings that could have been emails. You'll explain to the CEO why we can't "just add AI" to everything. You'll become emotionally attached to a linter config.

You'll make beautiful mockups that engineers will implement with... varying degrees of fidelity. You'll say "this doesn't match the design" at least 4 times per sprint. You'll learn that "pixel perfect" is a state of mind, not a reality.

You'll write blog posts at 2 AM. You'll answer the same question in Slack 47 times with the same enthusiasm each time. You'll become weirdly good at making GIFs. You'll explain what Klynt is to your family and they'll still say you "work in computers."
[HR said we couldn't publish this. Cowards.]

items.description: Our actual pitch deck. We can't believe they invested.
content: Slide 4: TAM slide with a completely made-up number
Slide 5: "Competition: everyone, but we are better"
Slide 6: "Team: two guys who met at YC"
Slide 7: Financial projections that were off by 400%
Somehow this worked. We raised $12M off this deck. The VC later admitted they invested because "the name was memorable." We choose to believe it was our compelling vision.

items.description: That time we emailed Tim Cook
content: Love the work you are doing with the iPhone. Big fan. We make product analytics software. Have you considered adding better analytics to Xcode? Also, would love to put Klynt on the App Store. Can you expedite the review?
Also — any chance you could mention us in the keynote? Even a small shoutout would be great.
[No response received. We waited 847 days before giving up.]

items.description: All the experiments that went nowhere
content: "Auto-generating release notes from commit messages","FAILED","Every release note was just 'fix stuff' 47 times"
"AI-powered support bot","FAILED","It told a customer to 'try turning it off and on again' for a billing question"
"Hedgehog-themed error pages","PARTIAL SUCCESS","Users loved them but they increased panic by 30%"
"Predictive churn analysis","FAILED","Predicted 100% churn for our own team after looking at our bug backlog"
"Voice-activated dashboards","FAILED","The mic kept picking up office dog barks as commands"
"QR codes on all error messages","FAILED","No one has ever scanned a QR code in their life, apparently"

items.redactedReason: This file has been redacted per Legal's request. If you are reading this, please stop. We mean it.
Seriously? You thought we would keep this? The CEO's browser history is stored on a separate server, in a bunker, guarded by a very judgmental sysadmin.

items.description: Our terms of service, translated to human English
content: 2. We won't sell your data. We are not Facebook.
3. If you break something, tell us. We don't judge (much).
4. We reserve the right to make dad jokes in the UI.
5. Free tier means free. No credit card trickery.
6. If you hit the free tier limits, either upgrade or get really good at data compression.
7. You own your data. We just hold it for you, like a very secure, very digital piggy bank.
8. No hedgehogs were harmed in the making of this software.
Legal team said this "isn't legally binding." We disagree. A pinky promise is legally binding in our hearts.

items.description: The DMs that started a company
content: "text": "yo what if we made analytics but like... not terrible?"
"text": "open source. developer-first. no sales team. just vibes."
"text": "that's the worst idea i've heard all week. let's do it."
"text": "yc application due in 3 days. think we can build a demo?"
We use Klynt to track how people use Klynt so we can make Klynt better. This is not a tongue twister; it is our actual job.
- We use Klynt analytics to analyze Klynt usage
- We use Klynt session replay to watch people use Klynt session replay
- We use Klynt feature flags to roll out new Klynt features
- We use Klynt experiments to test which Klynt experiments work best
2. The conversion rate of our conversion rate tracking
3. Whether our session replay replays sessions correctly
4. If our feature flag for feature flags is flagged correctly
*This document was deleted because it caused an infinite recursion in our analytics pipeline.*
